findings.title: North Star Metric: User Engagement
findings.description: The North Star metric for SmartNotify is the Daily Active Users (DAU), which measures the number of unique users engaging with the notification system daily. This metric reflects overall user engagement and the effectiveness of notification prioritization.
assumptions: DAU is a reliable indicator of product health.
recommendations: Monitor DAU trends weekly to assess engagement.

findings.title: Primary KPI: Notification Click-Through Rate (CTR)
findings.description: The notification click-through rate (CTR) measures the percentage of notifications that users interact with. This is a key performance indicator for assessing the effectiveness of notification content and delivery.
assumptions: Users are likely to click on relevant notifications.
CTR is a direct measure of notification effectiveness.
recommendations: Aim to increase CTR by optimizing notification content.

findings.title: Guardrail Metric: Unsubscribe Rate
findings.description: The unsubscribe rate measures the percentage of users opting out of notifications. This is a guardrail metric to ensure that user fatigue is not increasing, indicating that notifications are being perceived as spam.
assumptions: Users will unsubscribe if notifications are overwhelming.
A low unsubscribe rate indicates user satisfaction.

findings.title: Measurement Gap: User Preference Data
findings.description: There is a gap in data collection regarding user preferences for notification timing and content. This data is essential for improving notification batching and personalization.
assumptions: User preferences can be effectively captured through surveys or in-app feedback.

evidence.excerpt: ML model should learn per-user preferences for delivery timing.

recommendations: Implement a feedback mechanism to gather user preferences.

findings.title: Experiment Opportunity: Notification Delivery Latency
findings.description: Conduct an experiment to reduce notification delivery latency from the current p95 of 340ms to under 200ms. This will involve implementing asynchronous database writes and batch processing.
assumptions: Reducing latency will improve user engagement and satisfaction.

evidence.excerpt: Reduce notification delivery latency from p95 340ms to under 200ms.

recommendations: Set up A/B testing to measure the impact of latency reduction on user engagement.

summary: The analysis identifies the North Star metric as Daily Active Users, with supporting KPIs including Notification CTR and a guardrail metric of Unsubscribe Rate. Gaps in user preference data collection and opportunities for experiments to reduce delivery latency were also highlighted.
